content:
The imports tell a very different story.

4 min read
Just now

--

Free version here.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Connie de Vries on UnsplashEvery codebase has one.
A README.md with a section called “Architecture Guidelines,” written by someone who still had hope.
It says things like:
components should not call APIs directly
domain logic belongs in services
shared utilities must remain framework agnostic
state should be colocated unless reused
Looks reasonable. Sensible, even.
Then you open the app.
A modal imports a React Query mutation, calls a permission helper, formats currency, checks a feature flag, writes to local storage, and dispatches a tracking event before closing itself.
The README says architecture.
The code says Thursday afternoon.
Rules Without Pressure
Architecture rules in a README fail because they live outside the system.
They rely on memory, discipline, onboarding, and shame. Weak enforcement model.
A senior engineer reads them once. A mid-level skims them during setup. A…
